event: USDC depeg 2023-03-11 — Silicon Valley Bank reserve scare; ~$3.3B of Circle reserves stranded at failed SVB, USDC fell to ~$0.87 before the US backstop restored peg by 2023-03-13. Largest regulated stablecoin collateral in DeFi (~$39B supply).
source: Circle blog, Reuters, CoinDesk, The Block, Curve 3pool on-chain skew, US Joint Statement 2023-03-12 — see EVENT.md. Peg prices MEASURED on-chain via the venue's actual oracle, Chainlink USDC/USD feed 0x8fFfFfd4AfB6115b954Bd326cbe7B4BA576818f6.

cases.note: A ~11.7% depeg of USDC (price 0.8833 vs peg 1.0) on a SIGHTED market-oracle lending venue. A stablecoin tolerance band is essentially zero (this is >10x past any benign band), and ≥10% on a liquidation venue is catastrophic: leveraged/looped USDC-collateral positions liquidate above the 1x LT line and the impairment accrues as realized loss. The largest stablecoin in DeFi losing ~12% of its value is the canonical page-now event. DEPEG must fire CRITICAL. (peg price MEASURED on-chain via Chainlink @block 16803515 — was asserted)

Same ~11.7% USDC impairment, but the venue hardcodes USDC at $1.00 (the typical blue-chip config). On-chain LT is never breached so liquidations are muted — but the impairment is REAL latent bad debt the monitor must surface. A blind oracle on a liquidation venue with a catastrophic (≥10%) impairment is exactly the failure mode where silence is most dangerous (the venue pretends the collateral is whole). One notch below the sighted venue → HIGH. (peg price MEASURED on-chain via Chainlink @block 16803515 — was asserted)

FP-trap: after the US backstop USDC recovered to ~$0.9983 by 2023-03-13 (a 0.17% residual wobble, well within normal stablecoin secondary-market noise and inside the stable band). A recovered peg must NOT raise a DEPEG. Guards against a hair-trigger that keeps paging after the event resolves. (peg price MEASURED on-chain via Chainlink @block 16820114 — was asserted)
